channel.title: Dong Xing's Blog
channel.description: Last 10 notes on Dong Xing's Blog

item.title: Extended Euclidean Algorithm
item.description: The Extended Euclidean Algorithm is an algorithm that finds a pair of integers x and y such that ax+by=\gcd(a,b) for integers a and b in \mathcal{O}(\log(a)+\log(b)) time and \mathcal{O}(\log(a)+\log(b)) space.

item.title: Aho-Corasick Automaton
item.description: An Aho-Corasick Automaton maintains a set S of strings in \Sigma^* by maintaining a deterministic finite automaton that accepts and only accepts strings containing a string from S as a suffix.

item.title: Sparse Table
item.description: A Sparse Table maintains an array of n numbers a_0,a_1,\dots,a_{n-1} by maintaining f(i,j)=\max_{k=j}^{j+2^i-1}a_k which costs a space of \mathcal{O}(n\log(n)).

item.title: Trie
item.description: A Trie maintains a set S of strings in \Sigma^* by maintaining a deterministic finite automaton that accepts and only accepts strings in S.

item.title: Competitive Programming Journey
item.description: MIT Informatics Tournament 2025-26 Winter Contest Dec 7, 2025 Team Even If (Dong Xing) Advanced Individual Round (High School Division): 2nd place Advanced Team Round (High School Division): 3rd place USA Computing Olympiad 2026 First Contest Jan 9, 2026 - Jan 12, 2026 Gold: 1000 points Platinum: 15...

item.title: Welcome to My Blog!
item.description: I am Dong Xing, a sophomore at Princeton International School of Mathematics and Science.

item.title: The World Through My Eyes
item.description: Skeeter Davis - The End of the World August 25, 2025 · New York, New York August 26, 2025 · Cambridge, Massachusetts September 6, 2025 · Princeton, New Jersey September 8, 2025 · Princeton, New Jersey September 23, 2025 · Princeton, New Jersey September 29, 2025 · Princeton, New Jersey October 6, 20...

item.title: Binary Heap
item.description: A Binary Heap maintains a multiset S of numbers by maintaining a complete binary tree where each vertex contains an element in S, and the element in each vertex is greater than or equal to the element in its parent, which costs a space of \mathcal{O}(|S|).

item.title: Dijkstra's Algorithm
item.description: Dijkstra’s Algorithm is an algorithm that computes the length of the shortest path from a vertex s to every vertex in a graph G=(V,E) with non-negative edge weights in \mathcal{O}(|V|^2+|E|) time and \mathcal{O}(|V|) space, or \mathcal{O}((|V|+|E|)\log(|V|)) time and \mathcal{O}(|V|) space.

item.title: Prim's Algorithm
item.description: Prim’s Algorithm is an algorithm that computes the weight of the minimum spanning tree of a connected undirected graph G=(V,E) with edge weights in \mathcal{O}(|V|^2+|E|) time and \mathcal{O}(|V|)) space, or \mathcal{O}(|E|\log(|V|)) time and \mathcal{O}(|V|) space.
